$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:retcon_reading block (Retcon Planning coaching track, Door-B Selection step — F1). One block per candidate latent reading surfaced by the bug-or-feature abduction. `scores` is the Selection rubric: five named integer dimensions on a 1-5 scale (higher is better) — canon_coherence, payoff_density, agency_preservation, genre_fit, and coincidence_resistance (5 = no forced coincidences; 1 = rubber-reality over-fit, the failure mode the essay names). The subset schema checker (scripts/apodictic_artifacts.py) cannot express nested required keys or numeric ranges, so the five-dimension completeness and the 1-5 range are enforced in code by scripts/retcon_plan.py (R5), the same way the R3 fair-play gate lives in code. `coincidence_note` grounds the coincidence_resistance score (which incidental details the reading makes load-bearing); a surfaced reading lacking it raises the W3 over-fitting-guard advisory. `implied_targets` lists the DECLARED Retcon Target ids this reading maps onto once committed (Door B feeds Door A); it is empty for a not-yet-committed candidate, and any id present must resolve (R7). `reading` is a CLASS/label of the latent reading, never invented prose (the Firewall). See docs/retcon-planning.md.
title: APODICTIC retcon candidate reading (apodictic.retcon_reading.v1)
